key_insight: A clinically guided multimodal model combining ultrasound images, clinical data, and text improves fine-grained differentiation of breast fibroadenoma and phyllodes tumors.
clinical_relevance: Better preoperative classification of these look-alike fibroepithelial lesions may support more appropriate surgical planning and reduce misclassification of borderline phyllodes tumors as fibroadenomas.
method: DenseNet encodes ultrasound images, while separate lightweight encoders process clinical attributes and ultrasound report text.
A CLIP-inspired text branch plus clinical-conditioned adaptive modulation improves cross-modal alignment.
Cross-modal Transformer fusion and dual-path representation learning strengthen interaction across modalities.
evidence_signal: Using a pathology-confirmed FAPT-M dataset of 910 patients with patient-level five-fold cross-validation, the model reached 77.64% accuracy, 73.38% F1-score, and 89.74% AUC.
watch_item: The dataset is single-benchmark and retrospective, so broader external validation and real-world workflow testing are needed before clinical adoption.
paper_title: Multimodal Fusion for Fine-Grained Classification of Breast Fibroadenoma and Phyllodes Tumors
